info.description: The Denim Public API can be used by 3rd parties to sync companies, debtor relationships, payee relationships, and create invoices within Denim's payment platform. This enables streamlined operations and job invoice data between transportation management systems and Denim.
info.title: Denim Public API Reference Documentation

get.description: Returns a list of company search results. If a company entry has an existing Client-Payee or Client-Debtor relationship, that data will be returned, including the relationship ID that can be used in other API requests.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Mapping and Syncing Companies

parameters.description: The company name, MC Number, or DOT Number to search
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Type of company to sort by. Companies that match the type will be listed first. This is not a filter.
parameters.name: company_type

parameters.description: Filter to only include active companies
parameters.name: active

parameters.description: Sort results by specified field
parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Valid sort parameters for company results

parameters.description: The page of results to query.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: The number of entities per page of results.
parameters.name: per_page

get.description: Returns a list of all Factoring companies established in Denim's database. Return structure is similar to paginated results, however this will always return all factoring companies in 1 page.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Mapping and Syncing Companies

get.description: This retrieves the debtors that your company has a relationship with.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Mapping and Syncing Companies

parameters.description: Search string for debtor company name or MC Number.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Debtor Relationship Filters
parameters.name: filters

average_dbt_from.description: Average Days Beyond Terms lower bound

average_dbt_to.description: Average Days Beyond Terms upper bound

average_dso_from.description: Average Days Sales Outstanding lower bound

average_dso_to.description: Average Days Sales Outstanding upper bound

parameters.description: The page of results to query.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: The number of entities per page of results.
parameters.name: per_page

post.description: You can create a debtor relationship by linking to an existing approved company in our system (preferred) by passing the company_id found using the GET/companies endpoint. Alternatively, you can create a new company and client-debtor relationship simultaneously by sending the company attributes. Note that all credit requests are reviewed by our operations team for approval.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Mapping and Syncing Companies

existing_company_example.description: Creates a Client-Debtor Relationship using an existing company_id record.
existing_company_example.summary: Create Client-Debtor Relationship with Existing Company

new_company_with_credit_example.description: Add new company and client-debtor relationship with credit request. Note that credit requests are reviewed by our operations team.
new_company_with_credit_example.summary: Create a new company and Client-Debtor Relationship

get.description: A job is a collection of payables, receivables, and fees (obligations) associated with an invoice or purchase order.

parameters.description: Search by company name, amount, or job number
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Job statuses
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: When true, return only jobs that need attention (e.g. rejected, validation/verification failed, or have a client action required note)
parameters.name: needs_attention

parameters.description: The page of results to query.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: The number of entities per page of results.
parameters.name: per_page

post.description: Get a factoring fee quote for a Job.

delete.description: Deletes a Job in Pending or Draft status.

parameters.description: Job UUID
parameters.name: id

get.description: This retrieves a single job by UUID

parameters.description: Job UUID
parameters.name: id

post.description:
This endpoint enables adding documents to existing jobs.

This can accept either a json body with a publicly accessible `url`, `type` and `subtype`, or a multi-part form body including a `file` as the binary and `type` and `subtype`. In either case, the document will be uploaded and stored to our secure Google Cloud Platform storage.

parameters.description: Job UUID
parameters.name: job_id

post.description: Reverts a pending Job back to a draft status.

parameters.description: Job UUID
parameters.name: job_id

get.description: This retrieves the payees that your company has a relationship with.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Mapping and Syncing Companies

parameters.description: Search string for payee company name or MC Number.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description:
Payee Relationship Filters.

- status
- days to pay offset
- quick pay rate
    - Note: `quick_pay_rate` is in basis points (ex: 100 bps == %1.00)",
parameters.name: filters

days_to_pay_offset.description: Payee relationship days to pay offset

has_elected_payment_terms.description: Filter by whether the payee has elected payment terms

quick_pay_rate.description: Payee relationship quick pay rate, stored in basis points (ex: 100 bps == %1.00)

parameters.description: Filter for payee payment setting types
parameters.name: payment_types_filter

parameters.description: The page of results to query.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: The number of entities per page of results.
parameters.name: per_page

post.description: Creates a Client-Payee Relationship. Read the guide below for details. There are several different ways to create payees, whether it includes a factor assignment, etc. Important note: all factor assignments or updates create 'Requests' in our Request Center and these are all reviewed and approved by our operations team before becoming effective. This review is to ensure the proper payees receive payments and don't impact other payee setups.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Mapping and Syncing Companies

add_new_payee_example_without_factor.description: Invite a new payee (without a factor)
add_new_payee_example_without_factor.summary: Add a new payee without a factor

add_new_payee_with_factor_example.description: Invite a new payee with a factor
add_new_payee_with_factor_example.summary: Add a new payee with a pre-existing factor

add_new_payee_with_new_factor_example.description: Invite a new payee with a new factor not in the system
add_new_payee_with_new_factor_example.summary: Add a new payee with a new factor

existing_payee_example_without_factor.description: Add a relationship with an existing payee direct (without a factor)
existing_payee_example_without_factor.summary: Link existing payee without a factor

existing_payee_with_factor_example.description: Add a relationship with an existing payee with factoring relationship already setup
existing_payee_with_factor_example.summary: Link existing payee with a pre-existing factor relationship

existing_payee_with_new_factor_example.description: Add a relationship with an existing payee and set a new factor not in the system. New factor requests are reviewed by our operations team.
existing_payee_with_new_factor_example.summary: Link existing payee and set a new factor

post.description: Creates a request to change the factor assigned to the payee. These changes are reviewed by our operations team and the original factor will still be referenced until this request is reviewed and approved.

parameters.description: client_payee_relationship_id
parameters.name: id

update_with_existing_factor_example.description: Assign an existing factor to an existing payee relationship

update_with_new_factor_example.description: Assign a new factor to an existing payee relationship

schema.$ref: #/components/schemas/Update or set assigned factor on an existing payee relationship

requestBody.description: Update or set the assigned factor on an existing payee relationship

get.description: Transactions are payments incoming, outgoing, netting, and adjustments. Transactions are are generally applied towards obligations.

externalDocs.description: API Integration Guides: Working with Job Data

post.description: Create a new Job using our V2 API. Jobs created with this API can have certain Denim relationship IDs ommitted in favor of external IDs. Clients will then be able to utilitze the Denim Dashboard to review these Jobs and complete any mappings as necessary. Once a mapping between an external ID and a Denim ID has been completed, it will not need to be mapped for future Jobs.

2_factored_receivable_only_job.summary: Create a factored job with receivable only

value.notes: Notes for the Denim operations team.

7_payable_only_ltl_job.summary: Create a payable-only LTL job that references one or more receivable-only jobs

value.notes: Notes for the Denim operations team.

get.description: Returns a Job by UUID. This can return either a Job or a Job in Import Review. If the Job has been created, it will return a Job. If the Job has not yet been sumitted as a draft job and is under client_review, this will return a slimmer Job schema.

parameters.description: Job UUID
parameters.name: id

This can accept either a json body with a publicly accessible `url`, `type` and `subtype`, or a multi-part form body including a `file` as the binary and `type` and `subtype`. In either case, the document will be uploaded and stored to our secure Google Cloud Platform storage.

allocated_payables.description: [Not yet implemented] For receivable-only jobs, tracks the payables associated to the receivable. In particular for LTL shipments, this enables a receivable-only job to be associated with previous payable obligations.

allocated_receivables.description: [Not yet implemented] For payable-only jobs, tracks the receivables associated to the payable. In particular for LTL shipments, this enables a payable-only job to be associated with a future receivable obligation.

due_date.description: Date obligation is due in ISO format. The due_date should be within -60 and +120 days of the current datetime.

line_items.description:
Line Items associated with the obligation. Two requirements:
  1. base_amount line item must be provided, the rest should be accessorial_fee
  1. The sum of all line item amounts must add up to the total_amount of the receivable obligation

Payable Obligation.description: V2 Job payable obligation representing the payments to be made to carriers or contractors.

allocated_receivables.description: [Not yet implemented] For payable-only jobs, tracks the receivables associated to the payable. In particular for LTL shipments, this enables a payable-only job to be associated with a future receivable obligation.

documents.description: Documents associated with this obligation. Document type can be omitted as the type will be inferred by its presence with a receivable obligation.

type.description: Optional. Type will be set or overridden when included with a receivable (billing_paperwork) or payable (supporting_paperwork).

url.description: Full remote URL of document file to be uploaded. Content-Type header is required in response. Allowed Content-Type values: application/pdf, image/png and image/jpeg.

due_date.description: Date obligation is due in ISO format. The due_date should be within -60 and +120 days of the current datetime.

external_company_id.description: A unique identifier within the integrator's database (external to Denim). External company IDs will be able to go through a mapping experience within the Denim Dashboard to map external companies with Denim companies. Either an `external_company_id` OR `relationship_id` must be provided.

external_company_name.description: Name of the company associated with the `external_company_id`. This will show on the Import Review page to make it easier to map the external company to a Denim company.

relationship_id.description: A relationship_id within Denim. This should be either a ClientDebtor Relationship ID (receivable) or a ClientPayee Relationship ID (payable). Either an `external_company_id` OR `relationship_id` must be provided.

total_amount.description: Total amount of the obligation in cents

Payee Factor Company.description: Set the assigned factor for the payee.

factor_company_id.description: Factor company ID. Can be found using the Search Companies endpoint or use '0' for new a factor.

Payee Factor Company.title: Payee Factor Company

Debtor Company Credit Request.description: Credit request for a new debtor company
Debtor Company Credit Request.title: Debtor Company Credit Request

Add a document to a job.description: Provide a publically available `url` to a PDF, PNG, or JPEG documents associated with a job. Files are downloaded and then uploaded to our secure storage.

subtype.description: Billing or supporting paperwork subtype. Optional

type.description: Billing paperwork gets sent to debtor. Supporting paperwork allows us to pay the carrier.

url.description: Full remote URL of document file to be downloaded and uploaded to our secure storage. Content-Type header is required in response. Allowed Content-Type values: application/pdf, image/png and image/jpeg.

Add a document to a job.title: Add a document to a job

company.company_name: Example Company with Exisiting Client-Debtor Relationship and Credit Limit
Example Company with Exisiting Client-Payee Relationship and Factor

Factoring Option.description: Required. Option for if the job should be factored or not.
Factoring Option.title: Factoring Option

factor_company_name.description: Name of the payee's factoring company, if they have one

Obligation Line Item.description: Line Item associated with the obligation.

amount.description: Amount of the obligation in cents

Obligation Line Item.title: Obligation Line Item

Reference Number.description: Job reference number, must be unqiue to your company. Only alphanumeric and dashes allowed.
Reference Number.title: Reference Number

type.description: Optional. Type will be set or overridden when included with a receivable (billing_paperwork) or payable (supporting_paperwork).

url.description: Full remote URL of document file to be uploaded. Content-Type header is required in response. Allowed Content-Type values: application/pdf, image/png and image/jpeg.

is_debtor.description: Whether this company is a debtor type

is_factor.description: Whether this company is a factor type

is_payee.description: Whether this company is a payee type

last_payment_applied_at.description: The timestamp of the last payment received by this company

tags.description: A company in our system includes Payees, Debtors, and Factoring companies. Companies records are available globally in our ecosystem and shared by other clients.
tags.name: Companies

tags.description: A debtor is your customer or shipper. They are the entity that will be paying the invoices. Debtors are a type of Company in our ecosystem and are associated to our shared client through Client-Debtor Relationships.
tags.name: Debtors

tags.description: A payee is your contractor or carrier. They are the entity that needs to be paid for services. Payees are a type of Company in our ecosystem and are associated to our shared client through Client-Payee Relationships.
tags.name: Payees

tags.description: A job is a collection of obligations (payables, receivables, and fees) associated with an invoice or purchase order.
tags.name: Jobs

tags.description: Transactions are payments incoming, outgoing, netting, and adjustments. Transactions are are generally applied towards obligations.
tags.name: Transactions

tags.description: Version 2. A job is a collection of obligations (payables, receivables, and fees) associated with an invoice or purchase order.
tags.name: Jobs V2
